name: Chinese Chicken-Stuffed Peppers
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut a ½-inch thick slice from the side of each pepper and remove seeds. Place peppers in boiling water and boil for 5 minutes. Drain and set aside.
2. Coat a large skillet or wok with Pam cooking spray. Add sesame oil and heat over medium until hot. Add garlic and gingerroot; stir fry for 30 seconds.
3. Add carrots and green onions; stir fry for an additional 2 minutes, then remove from heat.
4. Add chicken and remaining ingredients to the skillet and stir well.
5. Spoon about three-quarters of the mixture into each reserved pepper.
6. Top with the reserved pepper slices.
7. Arrange peppers, cut side up, in a 10x6x2-inch baking dish. Cover and bake at 350°F for 30 minutes or until thoroughly heated.
PER SERVING: 231 calories, 16.4 g protein, 6.7 g fat, 25.7 carbohydrates, 98 g cholesterol, 3.1 mg iron, 366 mg sodium, 37 mg calcium.
